info.title: NetBox
info.x-itential-curated: Trimmed to 329 of 1194 upstream operations covering common CRUD for network automation: sites/locations/racks, device catalog and devices, interfaces and cabling, IPAM (prefixes/ranges/addresses/VLANs/VRFs), virtualization (clusters/VMs), tenancy, circuits, and tags/custom fields. See the repo README for the full scope and the full spec.
info.description:
NetBox is an open-source network source of truth platform for IP address management (IPAM) and data center infrastructure management (DCIM).

Authentication: API key in Authorization header — generate a token in NetBox under your user profile → API Tokens. Pass as Authorization: Token <api_token>.

Source: https://netboxlabs.com/docs/netbox/ | https://github.com/netbox-community/netbox

get.description:
This endpoint allows a user to determine what device (if any) is connected to a given peer device and peer
interface. This is useful in a situation where a device boots with no configuration, but can detect its neighbors
via a protocol such as LLDP. Two query parameters must be included in the request:

* `peer_device`: The name of the peer device
* `peer_interface`: The name of the peer interface

parameters.name: peer_device
parameters.description: The name of the peer device

parameters.name: peer_interface
parameters.description: The name of the peer interface
